title: Berry Connection
summary: The Berry connection is the U(1) connection on the line bundle of occupied states over regular momentum space, encoding phase transport of eigenstates along paths.
assumptions: The occupied state is isolated from the empty state over the region of interest.
A smooth local phase choice exists away from singularities.
regime: Adiabatic geometry of a gapped band subspace away from bad points.
scope: Lecture One U(1) Berry connection for a two-band problem with one filled state.

source_anchors.notes: Parallel transport of phase is the defining geometric picture.

symmetries: U(1) gauge redundancy in phase choice.
representation: Connection one-form on the occupied-state bundle.
motivation: This note exists because once the occupied states are organized into a line bundle, one still needs a local differential object that tracks how phase changes under adiabatic motion. Without the Berry connection, Berry phase and Berry curvature appear as disconnected formulas instead of geometric transport data.
prerequisite_story: Before reading this note, know that in the one-filled-band two-band problem the occupied states form a complex line bundle over the punctured momentum region. Choosing a normalized local section psi_p is a gauge choice, and changing psi_p by e^{i\chi(p)} psi_p is a U(1) redundancy rather than new physics.
local_context_story: In Witten Lecture One, the Berry connection is introduced immediately after the occupied-state line-bundle picture. The local parallel-transport rule along a path is the operational way to define how phases are compared at neighboring momenta.
forward_links_story: Once the connection is defined, its curvature F = dA becomes gauge invariant, the flux of F over a surrounding sphere becomes an integer node charge, and the same first-Chern-class viewpoint later reappears in Lecture Two as the TKNN invariant.
canonical_questions: Why is the Berry connection a connection on a line bundle instead of just a choice of phase convention?
How does the parallel-transport condition along a path determine the local one-form A?
How does A change under psi_p -> e^{i\chi(p)} psi_p, and why is that only gauge redundancy?
Why is the Berry connection not globally definable at a bad point?
scope_boundaries: This note treats the rank-one abelian case. Several filled bands require a non-abelian Berry connection.
The connection is defined on regular momentum regions or local patches, not globally through a singular band-touching point.

math_expressions.notes: Parallel-transport condition along a path; the connection is the object that enforces this phase convention.
Local gauge expression for the Berry connection one-form in a chosen local section.
Gauge transformation induced by psi_p -> e^{i\chi(p)} psi_p.

symbols.meaning: normalized occupied-state wavefunction at momentum p
Berry connection one-form in a local gauge

failure_modes: At bad points the bundle is singular and the U(1) connection is not globally defined.
retrieval_hints: Use for Berry phase, holonomy, or line-bundle questions.
